events.title: Henry I of England reigns
events.description: Henry I consolidates Norman rule and reforms administration.

events.title: Knights Templar founded
events.description: Military order founded to protect pilgrims in the Holy Land.

events.title: Chola decline and Pandya rise
events.description: Power shifts in South India as Pandyas and Hoysalas expand while Chola influence wanes.

events.title: Second Crusade
events.description: European kings campaign in the Levant and Iberia; largely unsuccessful.

events.title: Song dynasty technological diffusion
events.description: Gunpowder, movable type, and paper money spread and are refined during the Song era.

events.title: Murder of Thomas Becket
events.description: Archbishop of Canterbury killed; sparks conflict between church and crown.

events.title: Genghis Khan proclaimed
events.description: Temüjin unites Mongol tribes and begins empire-building across Eurasia.

events.title: Delhi Sultanate foundation
events.description: Qutb-ud-din Aibak establishes Muslim rule in northern India after Ghurid conquests.

events.title: Magna Carta sealed
events.description: Charter limiting royal power and protecting baronial rights in England.

events.title: Sack of Baghdad
events.description: Mongol forces destroy Baghdad, ending Abbasid political power.

events.title: Marco Polo travels to Asia
events.description: Venetian merchant travels the Silk Road and visits the Yuan court.

events.title: Ottoman Beylik founded
events.description: Ottoman state emerges in Anatolia and expands into the Balkans.

events.title: Mali Empire rises
events.description: Mali becomes a powerful West African empire controlling trade routes; later Mansa Musa's pilgrimage (1324) highlights wealth.

events.title: Black Death pandemic
events.description: Bubonic plague spreads across Eurasia, causing massive mortality and social change.

events.title: Vijayanagara Empire consolidates
events.description: South Indian empire grows as a Hindu bulwark against northern incursions and fosters temple culture and trade.

events.title: Timurid Empire begins
events.description: Timur establishes a Central Asian empire noted for conquest and cultural patronage centered on Samarkand.

events.title: Zheng He voyages
events.description: Ming treasure fleets sail across the Indian Ocean to Southeast Asia, India, Arabia, and East Africa, projecting Chinese maritime power.

events.title: Fall of Constantinople
events.description: Ottomans capture Constantinople, ending the Byzantine Empire and shifting trade routes between Europe and Asia.

events.title: Printing press spreads in Europe
events.description: Movable type printing accelerates information exchange and literacy across Europe.

events.title: Columbus reaches the Americas
events.description: Christopher Columbus lands in the Caribbean, initiating sustained contact between Europe and the Americas.

events.title: Babur founds Mughal Empire
events.description: Babur defeats Ibrahim Lodi at Panipat and establishes Mughal rule in northern India.

events.title: Martin Luther posts Theses
events.description: Protestant Reformation begins, challenging Catholic Church practices and reshaping European religion and politics.

events.title: Cortés conquers Aztecs
events.description: Spanish conquest leads to fall of Tenochtitlan and Aztec Empire.

events.title: Copernican heliocentrism published
events.description: Nicolaus Copernicus publishes a heliocentric model that challenges geocentric cosmology.
